name: Monty Alexander
abstract: Montgomery Bernard "Monty" Alexander OJ CD (born 6 June 1944) is a Jamaican American jazz pianist. His playing has a Caribbean influence and bright swinging feeling, with a strong vocabulary of bebop jazz and blues rooted melodies.

works.t: St. Louis Woman: Come Rain or Come Shine
Brigadoon: Almost Like Being in Love

concerts.label: Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: We Remember Erroll - A Tribute to Erroll Garner
Orange Colored Sky: Celebrating the Music of Nat King Cole
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Al Gafa
about_quotes.evidence: The piano player was Monty Alexander and the bass player was Bob Cranshaw. And the guitar player was me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Gafa interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 2, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: I did a trio with Monty Alexander. ... Yeah, the double-piano thing. We did that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Roland Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: this was the same year I met Monty Alexander. Monty came in that same night and played with us. I told him I said, “Look, this is my number, you come up to New York I’ll get you some work.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roland Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 15, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Ignacio Berroa
about_quotes.evidence: I remember I played with Monty Alexander and Monty told me, I said, “Monty, anything in particular that you wanted to hear?”
about_quotes.doc_title: Ignacio Berroa interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 22, 2021 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Taylor
about_quotes.evidence: I got a call from Monty Alexander who was booking Birdland and who I had known from his Royal Roost days and so forth. As a matter of fact I had known him from 52nd Street. I didn't know him very well but he had booked us concerts and I had met him there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Taylor — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: This is another one of those all-star sessions, including yourself, but I’m looking at Monty Alexander on tack piano... You talked about Quincy... This is another one of those all-star sessions
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
